privileged_info_summary: The task operates entirely on synthetic data generated at runtime by setup_task.sh. It injects 32 unorganized bookmarks directly into the Chrome profile and creates non-compliant Preferences and Local State files. The target configuration instructions are synthetically written to ~/Desktop/factory_workstation_standard.txt. The task.json contains no metadata claims. I have identified two significant bugs in the verifier.py logic: first, it attempts to verify custom search engines by parsing the Preferences JSON file, but Chrome stores custom site search keywords in the Web Data SQLite database, causing this check to fail under normal UI interaction. Second, when verifying bookmark categorization, the verifier only checks the contents of the 'Quality & Standards' and 'Components & Suppliers' folders, completely ignoring whether domains are placed correctly in the remaining three required folders.
